tldr: You're shipping fresh creative consistently and running 13 distinct concepts — that's healthy and clears Andromeda's diversity floor. The bigger issue is that a cluster of your Leo Gregory tee carousels and the 'Out with the old. In with the new.' new-drop posts have been live 60–90 days with identical body copy across languages, and they're now in the danger band while your spring '26 SS26 launches are doing the heavy lifting.

benchmark.context: Streetwear brands at your scale should be cycling hero creative every ~14 days, with the highest-volume accounts pushing closer to 10. You're in good company on refresh cadence — the problem is a tail of evergreen carousels from Q1 that nobody retired when SS26 shipped.

ads.headline: Leo Gregory Graphic T-Shirt White - XXXL / White
ads.body: Weekend Offender: Fresh tones and statement graphics designed with sharp focus on functionality and detail.
drivers: Live for 90 days — six full refresh cycles past your 14-day window for a hero SKU

The exact same body copy ('Fresh tones and statement graphics...') runs verbatim on at least eight other Leo Gregory tee variants in English, French, German and Italian — Andromeda is treating these as one cluster
The headline is just the SKU + size + colour, which reads as a feed entry, not an ad — no hook, no benefit, no reason to stop scrolling

ads.headline: Weekend Offender
ads.body: Out with the old. In with the new. Weekend Offender's new drop is here.
drivers: Live for 90 days promoting a 'new drop' that is no longer new — the spring '26 collection has since launched in parallel, undercutting the claim
The same 'Out with the old. In with the new.' line is recycled across UK, German and French versions still running side-by-side
Headline field is just the brand name doing nothing — pair that with a generic body and there's zero specificity to anchor against the SS26 work

ads.headline: Leo Gregory Graphic T-Shirt White - XL / White
ads.body: Weekend Offender : Des tons frais et des graphismes affirmés, conçus avec un sens aigu de la fonctionnalité et du détail.
drivers: 90 days live — French translation of the same Leo Gregory tee carousel that's been the workhorse since February
Body copy is functionally identical to the English, German and Italian variants — five-language clones of one concept compressed by Andromeda's retrieval clustering
SKU-as-headline pattern repeats — no editorial reason to click beyond raw product listing

ads.headline: Leo Gregory Graphic T-Shirt White - XL / White
ads.body: Weekend Offender: Frische Töne und markante Grafiken, entwickelt mit klarem Fokus auf Funktionalität und Details.
drivers: 90 days live in the German market — past the natural decay curve for any single tee SKU
Direct German translation of an English ad that's also been running 90 days; Andromeda reads these as the same concept with localisation
No seasonal angle, no founder voice, no UGC layer — just product + size + colour, which is the lowest-leverage version of streetwear creative

ads.headline: Weekend Offender
ads.body: Raus mit dem Alten. Rein mit dem Neuen. Der neue Drop von Weekend Offender ist da.
drivers: 87 days live — the German 'new drop' announcement is no longer announcing anything new
Headline is just 'Weekend Offender' — the brand name carrying the entire top-of-ad real estate is wasted impressions for a cold prospecting placement
You shipped a fresh image-format version of this exact German new-drop message three days ago — the old carousel should have been paused when the new asset went live

hero_concept.concept_name: Sheffield — City Series, On The Move
hero_concept.headline: City Series 8. Out now.
hero_concept.primary_text:
The City Series is how we tell where we've been. Sheffield is the latest — black cotton jersey, red graphic, every detail built for the way you actually wear a tee on a Saturday.

No studio shot. No filler. Just the next chapter of a series that started with the cities we grew up on.

Free socks with orders over £70 this week.
hero_concept.visual_direction: The Sheffield tee shot in a real Northern English urban context — wet pavement, brick terrace, late-afternoon light — laid flat or hung on a railing rather than on a model, so the graphic and colourway are the hero.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Almost every recent ad either runs the brand name as the headline or the raw SKU string — there's no creative that names a specific product story or city the way City Series does in your catalogue. This concept also breaks the carousel-and-collection-page habit by anchoring on one SKU with editorial weight, which is what your spring '26 video work is doing well and your evergreen statics are missing.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a wet Northern English urban setting with the tee laid flat across weathered red brick beside a damp cobbled side-street, with low-angle late-afternoon golden light raking across the fabric and casting soft long shadows. Contemporary streetwear aesthetic, moody but clean, editorial product-first composition with cinematic colour grading. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL — preserve the exact deep black colorway of the cotton jersey AND every printed graphic, color, and text element exactly as shown on the chest, including the red 'Sheffield' / 'City Series 8' graphic typography and any secondary print elements in their exact placement, scale, and color — strictly NO color shift to grey or charcoal, NO red shift to orange or pink, NO removing of print, NO altering of graphic colors or typography. STRUCTURE — preserve the exact construction: crew neckline, short sleeves, regular straight fit, printed brand label at back of neck if visible. PRESERVE the front-chest printed graphic exactly — every illustration, every text element, all colors, same placement and scale. Do NOT add additional graphics or text not present in the reference, do NOT remove the chest print, do NOT alter the graphic, do NOT change neckline shape or sleeve length, do NOT add pockets, do NOT change to a fitted or oversized silhouette beyond what the reference shows. Render the product's metal, color, finish, fabric, weave, stones, surface treatment, AND every visible construction detail (silhouette, closures, hardware count, pockets, sleeves, neckline, printed graphics) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the metal type, do NOT shift the color temperature, do NOT introduce tones not present in the reference, do NOT add or remove buttons, snaps, zippers, pockets, straps, or any hardware, do NOT alter the silhouette, do NOT remove or modify printed graphics or text on the garment. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself — text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.

next_step.urgency: Four of your top ads pass their refresh window inside ten days.
next_step.headline: Walk through the kill list with me
next_step.body: I'll show you which of the 90-day Leo Gregory and 'new drop' carousels to pause this week and what shape of replacement creative would carry the next 30 days. Thirty minutes, no deck.

method_note: We went through every live ad in your Meta Ad Library on May 3, 2026 and scored each on six fatigue signals against streetwear refresh windows; we don't have your account-side CPM, CTR or ROAS, so all calls are based on public creative patterns and days-running.
